Function Documentation

EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE const unsigned char * get_font_default_ptr (void )

Get the pointer to the embedded default font data

Returns

Pointer to font data

Definition at line 313 of file mirror.c.

313                                                 {
314   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set
315   int matrix_rain_val = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
316   log_info("[get_font_default_ptr] CALLED: matrix_rain=%d, g_font_matrix_resurrected=%p, g_font_default=%p",
317            matrix_rain_val, (void *)g_font_matrix_resurrected, (void *)g_font_default);
318   if (matrix_rain_val) {
319     log_info("[get_font_default_ptr] RETURNING matrix font");
320     return g_font_matrix_resurrected;
321   }
322   log_info("[get_font_default_ptr] RETURNING default font");
323   return g_font_default;
324 }




References g_font_default,
    g_font_matrix_resurrected, GET_OPTION, and
  log_info.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  unsigned int get_font_default_size (void )


Get the size of the embedded default font


Returns


Font data size in bytes


Definition at line 331 of file mirror.c.


331                                          {
332   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set
333   if (GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
334     return (unsigned int)g_font_matrix_resurrected_size;
335   }
336   return (unsigned int)g_font_default_size;
337 }






References g_font_default_size,
    g_font_matrix_resurrected_size, and GET_OPTION.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  void mirror_cleanup (void )


Definition at line 85 of file mirror.c.


85                           {
86   if (g_digital_rain) {
87     digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
88     g_digital_rain = NULL;
89   }
90   if (g_last_rain_output) {
91     SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
92     g_last_rain_output = NULL;
93   }
94   term_renderer_glyph_cache_cleanup();
95   options_state_destroy();
96   platform_destroy();
97 }






References digital_rain_destroy(),

    and term_renderer_glyph_cache_cleanup().






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  char * mirror_convert_frame (uint8_t * rgba_data, int src_width, int
  src_height)


Definition at line 104 of file mirror.c.


104                                                                               {
105   if (!rgba_data || src_width <= 0 || src_height <= 0) {
106     return NULL;
107   }
108 
109   g_frame_count++;
110   // Derive a time value from frame count using actual target FPS from options
111   int target_fps = (int)GET_OPTION(fps);
112   if (target_fps <= 0) {
113     target_fps = 60; // Default to 60 FPS if not set
114   }
115   float time_seconds = (float)g_frame_count / (float)target_fps;
116 
117   // Get current settings from options or terminal detection
118   int dst_width = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
119   int dst_height = (int)terminal_get_effective_height();
120   color_filter_t filter = (color_filter_t)GET_OPTION(color_filter);
121   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
122   if (g_frame_count <= 3 || g_frame_count % 120 == 0) {
123     log_debug("frame=%u color_filter=%d color_mode=%d", g_frame_count, (int)filter, (int)color_mode);
124   }
125   if (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
126     color_mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR; // xterm.js supports truecolor
127   }
128   palette_type_t palette_type = (palette_type_t)GET_OPTION(palette_type);
129   bool preserve_aspect_ratio = true; // Preserve webcam aspect ratio
130   bool stretch = false;              // Don't stretch - maintain proportions
131 
132   // Build terminal capabilities structure with user's color mode
133   terminal_capabilities_t caps = {0}; // Zero-initialize all fields first
134   caps.color_level = color_mode;
135   caps.capabilities = 0;
136   caps.color_count = (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_NONE)  ? 0
137                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_16)  ? 16
138                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_256) ? 256
139                                                       : 16777216;
140   caps.utf8_support = true;
141   caps.detection_reliable = true;
142   caps.render_mode = (render_mode_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
143   caps.wants_background = (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND);
144   caps.wants_padding = true;
145   caps.palette_type = palette_type;
146   caps.desired_fps = 60;
147   caps.color_filter = filter;
148 
149   // Convert RGBA to RGB (strip alpha channel) and optionally flip horizontally
150   rgb_pixel_t *rgb_pixels = SAFE_MALLOC(src_width * src_height * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t), rgb_pixel_t *);
151   if (!rgb_pixels) {
152     return NULL;
153   }
154 
155   bool flip_x = GET_OPTION(flip_x);
156   bool flip_y = GET_OPTION(flip_y);
157   for (int y = 0; y < src_height; y++) {
158     for (int x = 0; x < src_width; x++) {
159       int src_x = flip_x ? (src_width - 1 - x) : x;
160       int src_y = flip_y ? (src_height - 1 - y) : y;
161       int src_idx = (src_y * src_width + src_x) * 4;
162       int dst_idx = y * src_width + x;
163 
164       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].r = rgba_data[src_idx + 0];
165       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].g = rgba_data[src_idx + 1];
166       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].b = rgba_data[src_idx + 2];
167       // Alpha (rgba_data[src_idx + 3]) is discarded
168     }
169   }
170 
171   // Apply color filter to pixels if needed (except rainbow - handled in ANSI stage)
172   if (filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE && filter != COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
173     // color_filter operates on packed RGB24 format
174     uint8_t *rgb24 = (uint8_t *)rgb_pixels;
175     int stride = src_width * 3;
176     apply_color_filter(rgb24, src_width, src_height, stride, filter, time_seconds);
177   }
178 
179   // Create image structure
180   image_t img = {.w = src_width, .h = src_height, .pixels = rgb_pixels, .alloc_method = IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD};
181 
182   // Get palette characters based on selected palette type
183   const char *palette_chars;
184   switch (palette_type) {
185   case PALETTE_BLOCKS:
186     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS;
187     break;
188   case PALETTE_DIGITAL:
189     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL;
190     break;
191   case PALETTE_MINIMAL:
192     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL;
193     break;
194   case PALETTE_COOL:
195     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_COOL;
196     break;
197   case PALETTE_CUSTOM:
198     // Custom palette uses user-provided characters from options
199     palette_chars = GET_OPTION(palette_custom);
200     if (!palette_chars || palette_chars[0] == '\0') {
201       palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD; // Fallback
202     }
203     break;
204   case PALETTE_STANDARD:
205   default:
206     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD;
207     break;
208   }
209 
210   // Convert to ASCII using capability-aware function
211   char *ascii_output = ascii_convert_with_capabilities(&img, dst_width, dst_height, &caps, preserve_aspect_ratio,
212                                                        stretch, palette_chars);
213 
214   // Clean up
215   SAFE_FREE(rgb_pixels);
216 
217   if (!ascii_output) {
218     log_error("ascii_convert_with_capabilities returned NULL");
219     return NULL;
220   }
221 
222   // Apply rainbow color filter to ANSI output by replacing RGB values
223   // This preserves character selection while applying rainbow colors
224   if (filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
225     char *rainbow_output = rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(ascii_output, time_seconds);
226     if (rainbow_output) {
227       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
228       ascii_output = rainbow_output;
229     }
230   }
231 
232   bool matrix_rain = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
233   if (matrix_rain) {
234     // Initialize digital rain context if needed or dimensions changed
235     if (!g_digital_rain || g_digital_rain->num_columns != dst_width || g_digital_rain->num_rows != dst_height) {
236       if (g_digital_rain) {
237         digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
238       }
239       if (g_last_rain_output) {
240         SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
241         g_last_rain_output = NULL;
242       }
243       g_digital_rain = digital_rain_init(dst_width, dst_height);
244       if (!g_digital_rain) {
245         log_error("Failed to initialize digital rain effect");
246         return ascii_output; // Return without effect on error
247       }
248       g_last_rain_update_time = emscripten_get_now();
249     }
250 
251     // Update color from active filter (allows filter changes after initialization)
252     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(g_digital_rain, filter);
253 
254     // Time-based updates: only update effect every RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS
255     double current_time = emscripten_get_now();
256     double elapsed_ms = current_time - g_last_rain_update_time;
257 
258     if (elapsed_ms >= RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS) {
259       // Calculate actual delta time in seconds
260       float delta_time = (float)(elapsed_ms / 1000.0);
261       g_last_rain_update_time = current_time;
262 
263       // Apply effect and cache a copy
264       char *rain_output = digital_rain_apply(g_digital_rain, ascii_output, delta_time);
265       if (rain_output) {
266         // Free old cached output
267         if (g_last_rain_output) {
268           SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
269         }
270         // Cache a copy (we'll return the original rain_output)
271         g_last_rain_output = strdup(rain_output);
272         // Return the rain output (caller will free it)
273         SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
274         ascii_output = rain_output;
275       }
276     } else if (g_last_rain_output) {
277       // Use cached output
278       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
279       ascii_output = strdup(g_last_rain_output);
280     }
281   } else {
282     // Clean up digital rain context if it exists but effect is disabled
283     if (g_digital_rain) {
284       digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
285       g_digital_rain = NULL;
286     }
287     if (g_last_rain_output) {
288       SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
289       g_last_rain_output = NULL;
290     }
291     g_last_rain_update_time = 0.0;
292   }
293 
294   return ascii_output;
295 }






References apply_color_filter(),
    ascii_convert_with_capabilities(), rgb_pixel_t::b,
    terminal_capabilities_t::capabilities,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_count,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_filter, COLOR_FILTER_NONE,
    COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW, terminal_capabilities_t::color_level,
    terminal_capabilities_t::desired_fps,
    terminal_capabilities_t::detection_reliable,
    digital_rain_apply(), digital_rain_destroy(),
    digital_rain_init(), digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(),
    rgb_pixel_t::g, GET_OPTION, IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD,
    log_debug, log_error, PALETTE_BLOCKS,
    PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS, PALETTE_CHARS_COOL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL, PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD, PALETTE_COOL, PALETTE_CUSTOM,
    PALETTE_DIGITAL, PALETTE_MINIMAL, PALETTE_STANDARD,
    terminal_capabilities_t::palette_type, rgb_pixel_t::r,
    RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS, rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::render_mode, RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, TERM_COLOR_16,
    TERM_COLOR_256, TERM_COLOR_AUTO, TERM_COLOR_NONE,
    TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR, terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::utf8_support,
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_background, and
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_padding.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  void mirror_free_string (char * ptr)


Definition at line 298 of file mirror.c.


298                                    {
299   SAFE_FREE(ptr);

References SAFE_FREE.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  int mirror_init_with_args (const char * args_json)


Initialize mirror mode with command-line style arguments


Parameters


args_json JSON array of argument strings, e.g.
  ["mirror", "--width", "80",
  "--height", "40"]


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 47 of file mirror.c.


47                                                  {
48   // Initialize platform layer
49   asciichat_error_t err = platform_init();
50   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
51     return -1;
52   }
53 
54   // Initialize logging to stderr (console.error in browser)
55   log_init(NULL, LOG_DEBUG, true, false);
56   // Use debug-style format in WASM so browser console shows full log headers
57   log_set_format(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG, false);
58 
59   // Parse space-separated arguments
60   char *args_copy = NULL;
61   char *argv[64] = {NULL};
62   int argc = wasm_parse_args(args_json, argv, 64, &args_copy);
63   if (argc < 0) {
64     return -1;
65   }
66 
67   // Initialize options (sets up RCU, defaults, etc.)
68   err = options_init(argc, argv);
69   free(args_copy);
70 
71   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
72     return -1;
73   }
74 
75   // Log option values after initialization
76   log_info("[mirror_init_with_args] After options_init: argc=%d, matrix_rain=%d", argc, GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
77 
78   // Initialize ANSI color code generation (dec3 cache for RGB values)
79   ansi_fast_init();
80 
81   return 0;
82 }






References ansi_fast_init(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    GET_OPTION, LOG_DEBUG, log_info, log_init(),
    log_set_format(), OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG,
    options_init(), platform_init(), and
  wasm_parse_args().
